topStories.description: U.K. Foreign Secretary has been in touch to express the country’s solidarity with partners in the region, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, and Kuwait — many of whom have been targeted with Iranian strikes, which we strongly condemn, says Shalini Medepalli

topStories.title: Did Khamenei doubt son Mojtaba as successor? ‘Unqualified, not very bright’: US intel briefed Trump - Report
topStories.description: Earlier this month, Mojtaba Hosseini Khamenei – the second son of slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei – was elected as Iran's new supreme leader. At that time, reports mentioned that the decision was allegedly taken under pressure from the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)

topStories.title: U.S. attitude to the Kurdish question reflects colonial mindset, says Nilufer Koc
topStories.description: Spokesperson of Kurdistan National Congress says the U.S. made the proposal about Kurdish fighters without broader consultation with the Kurds

topStories.title: Arvind Kejriwal In Supreme Court, Challenges Decision In Excise Policy Case
topStories.description: On Monday, Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma is expected to hear the CBI's petition challenging the release of Arvind Kejriwal, his former Deputy Manish Sisodia and a few others accused in the excise...

topStories.title: US Senator Van Hollen: Trump ‘has no idea where this war is going’
topStories.description: Democratic US Senator Chris Van Hollen argues that US-Israel war on Iran ‘is making America and our allies less safe'.

topStories.title: US-Israel, Iran conflict: Trump's appeal to allies to send ships to reopen Hormuz brings no promises yet
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump urges allies to send warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open amid the US-Israel war in Iran. However, no commitments have been made so far. Iran’s IRGC closed the Strait, raising oil to $120/barrel. IEA to release 400 million barrels from emergency stockpiles

topStories.title: Saptak Talwar Clinches Indorama Ventures Open 2026 Title
topStories.description: Saptak Talwar secured a commanding victory at the US$ 300,000 Indorama Ventures Open Golf Championship on Sunday.

topStories.title: Iran wants deal to end war? Abbas Araghchi pushes back on Trump claims, says ‘never asked for ceasefire’
topStories.description: This week, US President Donald Trump said Iran wanted a deal, but that he was not prepared to make one on current terms, without giving further details. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reacted to the President's claim.

topStories.title: US-Israel, Iran conflict: IEA says over 400 million barrels of emergency oil to start flowing soon amid tensions
topStories.description: The IEA is releasing 400 million barrels of emergency oil to global markets as the US-Israel conflict with Iran disrupts supplies. Member countries in the Americas, Asia Oceania, and Europe will provide government and industry stocks. The move aims to calm markets after crude hit $120/barrel.

topStories.title: Iran War News Highlights: IRGC Vows To 'Kill' Netanyahu, Trump Seeks Help To Reopen Hormuz
topStories.description: President Donald Trump called on allied countries to deploy warships to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

topStories.title: Wiz investor unpacks Google’s $32B acquisition
topStories.description: Shardul Shah of Index Ventures walks us through Google's biggest acquisition ever.

topStories.title: Wild US Weather Halts Flights, Dumps Snow and Knocks Out Power
topStories.description: Blizzards, wildfires and thunderstorms are swirling toward the eastern US having knocked out power to thousands and grounded hundreds of flights across the Midwest.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls for naval coalition to open Strait of Hormuz: Can it work?
dailyNews.description: Analysts say US president's solution to secure the strait is fraught with problems.

dailyNews.title: Team USA Paralympic sled hockey completes clean sweep of Canada at Winter Games
dailyNews.description: Team USA made Paralympic history with its fifth consecutive sled hockey gold medal, defeating Canada, 6-2, in a dominant performance in Italy.

dailyNews.title: Oscars 2026 brings star-studded red-carpet fashion led by Heidi Klum and Rose Byrne
dailyNews.description: Oscars red carpet 2026 delivered glamorous fashion as Heidi Klum wore champagne crystal gown, Rose Byrne stunned in black mermaid dress, and Felicity Jones chose pale yellow tulle.

dailyNews.title: Lamar Odom reveals Kobe Bryant's mysterious message from beyond in 'vivid' dream encounter
dailyNews.description: Lamar Odom revealed a vivid dream where the late Los Angeles Lakers legend Kobe Bryant delivered a surprising message about the afterlife during their competition together.

dailyNews.title: Schiff, Booker deflect on shutdown blame amid terror concerns, thousands of DHS workers without pay
dailyNews.description: Democratic senators Schiff and Booker deflected blame over the DHS shutdown amid terror concerns in the U.S. on Sunday, as workers have gone weeks without pay.

dailyNews.title: Pope Leo urges war leaders to halt fighting after deadly strike on school sparks outrage
dailyNews.description: Pope Leo XIV called Sunday for an immediate ceasefire in the war involving Iran, urgently urging world leaders to halt violence after deadly strikes hit schools and civilian areas.

dailyNews.title: Spring break crackdown: Southern towns roll out alcohol bans and new restrictions
dailyNews.description: Spring break destinations implement new safety rules as travel season approaches. Panama City Beach introduces overnight closures while other cities adjust policies.

dailyNews.title: Illegal immigrant released under Biden charged with groping female students at Virginia high school
dailyNews.description: Mother of three students speaks out about alleged sexual assault case at Virginia high school, criticizing delayed notification and school district policies.

dailyNews.title: CarGurus breach linked to ShinyHunters exposes 12.4M records
dailyNews.description: CarGurus users face a security risk after the ShinyHunters hacking group allegedly leaked 12.4 million records containing names, addresses and finance data.

dailyNews.title: Oprah Winfrey fires back at internet trolls mocking her '90-year-old' walk at Paris Fashion Week
dailyNews.description: Oprah Winfrey hilariously explains why she appeared to walk slowly at Paris Fashion Week, revealing she couldn't see through non-prescription sunglasses.

dailyNews.title: Tori Spelling sets the record straight on 30 years of plastic surgery rumors
dailyNews.description: Tori Spelling opens up about devastating plastic surgery rumors that started when she was just 17 on "90210" and continue to hurt her feelings at 52.

dailyNews.title: FCC chair Brendan Carr warns broadcasters must 'correct course' on 'news distortions' before license renewals
dailyNews.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr warned broadcasters running "fake news" could lose licenses during renewals, citing public interest requirements and media accountability.

topStories.title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
topStories.description: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.

topStories.title: After Trump’s Call to Send Warships to Strait of Hormuz, Nations Respond With Caution
topStories.description: President Trump has urged China, Britain, France, Japan and South Korea to send warships to help reopen the waterway, even though they were not involved in the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran.

topStories.title: Markwayne Mullin, Trump’s Homeland Security Pick, Got Wealthier Stock Trading in Congress
topStories.description: Markwayne Mullin’s financial dealings take on new importance as the Senate considers his nomination to lead an agency whose budget has vastly expanded.

topStories.title: Trump Administration Turns to Migrant Workers to Help Farm Labor Shortage
topStories.description: As the president’s immigration policies squeeze an already tight supply of farm labor, the Trump administration is making it cheaper to hire foreign farmworkers.

topStories.title: Crenshaw says Trump is "doing what's needed" in the Middle East conflict
topStories.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw, whose term as representative of Texas's 2nd congressional district is set to end following his loss in the Republican primary earlier this month​, appeared on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan​" on Sunday.

topStories.title: Trump thanks TSA agents working with no pay amid government shutdown
topStories.description: TSA officers faced their first full missed paycheck Friday.

dailyNews.title: Trump thanks TSA agents working with no pay amid government shutdown
dailyNews.description: TSA officers faced their first full missed paycheck Friday.

dailyNews.title: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says broadcast licenses are not a "property right"
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with CBS News Saturday, Federal Communications Chair Brendan Carr doubled down on his warning that broadcast licenses could be revoked amid President Trump's criticisms of media coverage of the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump economic adviser Kevin Hassett says of Iran war funding: "Right now, we've got what need"
dailyNews.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that so far, the war with Iran has cost $12 billion, adding that "right now, we've got what we need" on funding as lawmakers are preparing for a possible supplemental funding request from the White House.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighing options to strike Iran's critical oil hub, UN Ambassador Waltz says
dailyNews.description: Israel says it has killed two senior Iranian intelligence officials as oil loading reportedly resumes in the UAE's Fujairah port following a fire.

dailyNews.title: Democrats blast FCC Chair Carr's broadcast license threats as anti-First Amendment, 'totalitarian'
dailyNews.description: Carr on Saturday blasted broadcasters shortly after President Donald Trump called reports that Iran struck five U.S. tanker planes "fake news."

dailyNews.title: Trump says Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire but he's not ready to make a deal
dailyNews.description: In a wide-ranging interview, President Donald Trump said he’s not ready to make a deal to end the war with Iran, despite Iran’s willingness to do so.

dailyNews.title: Trump says there’s been ‘total obliteration’ of Iran militarily
dailyNews.description: President Trump told NBC News’ Kristen Welker in a nearly half hour phone conversation that Iran has been “militarily defeated” and he believes other countries will help to secure passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'
dailyNews.description: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Appointee wants to replace White House columns with the ones Trump prefers
dailyNews.description: The head of a federal arts commission is proposing the more ornate Corinthian style for the nearly 200-year-old columns at the building’s front entrance.
